title: Commit Milestone 1 & 2 work
description: Commit all M1/M2 implementation files, update milestones.md status, tag as v0.2.0, and push to origin

title: Phase 6: Docker & infrastructure
description: Update Dockerfile: add ffmpeg + yt-dlp to runner stage, create /app/data/videos dir. Update docker-compose.yml with new env vars (OLLAMA_URL, OLLAMA_MODEL, LLM_MODE). Update .env.example. Update config.ts with optional env vars. Update csp.js for media-src self.

title: Phase 4: API routes
description: New routes/videoJobs.ts: POST /api/dishes/import-video-url (rate limited 2/min), GET /api/jobs/:id, GET /api/dishes/:id/video (range requests), DELETE /api/dishes/:id/video, GET /api/settings/ollama-status. Update settings service/routes for new fields. Update dishes routes for video fields.

title: Phase 5: Frontend video player & import flow
description: New VideoPlayer.tsx (HTML5 video, poster thumbnail). Update RecipeImportModal with smart URL detection (video URLs → video import flow with job polling). Update DishesPage with inline video player. New AI & Video settings section (LLM mode, Ollama URL/model, n8n webhook, storage limit). Update api.ts client.

title: Phase 3: LLM integration service
description: New services: ollama.ts (health check, recipe extraction via /api/generate, 60s timeout, graceful failure), recipeExtraction.ts (router for llmMode: disabled/direct/n8n), n8nLlm.ts (deferred). Reads yt-dlp .info.json for description text.

title: Phase 1: DB schema + shared types
description: Schema migration: add video columns to dishes (localVideoFilename, videoThumbnailFilename, videoSize, videoDuration), LLM settings to appSettings (ollamaUrl, ollamaModel, llmMode, n8nWebhookUrl, videoStorageLimitMb), new videoJobs table. Update Drizzle schema, shared types, shared Zod schemas.

title: Phase 2: Video download service
description: New services: videoDownload.ts (yt-dlp subprocess wrapper with mp4 output, info.json, thumbnails, 500MB limit, 10min timeout) and videoJobs.ts (job queue: create/process/get with background processing, progress tracking).

title: Video download & LLM recipe extraction
description: Hybrid approach: yt-dlp embedded in container for video download, LLM has 3 configurable modes (disabled/direct Ollama/n8n). Smart auto-detect in import modal. Default model: gemma4-e4b. Storage: 500MB/video, 10GB total.
close_reason: All 6 phases complete, branch ready for PR

title: fix: bump pnpm overrides for tar, esbuild, bn.js vulnerabilities
description: pnpm audit reports 3 vulns: tar <=7.5.9 (high, bcrypt chain), esbuild <=0.24.2 (moderate, drizzle-kit chain), bn.js <4.12.3 (moderate, @fastify/jwt chain). Fix: update tar override to >=7.5.10, add esbuild >=0.25.0 and bn.js >=4.12.3 overrides in root package.json.

title: fix: timezone bug — Today page shows wrong date after ~9 PM
description:
Root cause: new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0] always returns UTC date. At 9:55 PM EST (UTC-5), UTC is already the next day, causing TodayPage, WeekPage, GroceryPage, and PantryPage to show the wrong date.

Fix required:
1. Frontend (4 pages): replace toISOString date extraction with local date methods (getFullYear/getMonth/getDate). Add localDateStr() helper to apps/web/src/lib/utils.ts.
   - apps/web/src/pages/GroceryPage.tsx:11 - getTodayDateStr()
   - apps/web/src/pages/TodayPage.tsx:28
   - apps/web/src/pages/WeekPage.tsx:49
   - apps/web/src/pages/PantryPage.tsx:17,21

2. Backend: formatDate() in apps/api/src/services/menus.ts:26 uses toISOString() which is UTC. Fix to use local date methods so it respects TZ env var. Same for default today param in apps/api/src/services/suggestions.ts:20,39,83.

3. docker-compose.yml: Add TZ env var to production service (and dev service) so users can configure their timezone:
   - TZ: ${TZ:-}  (empty default = UTC)

Node.js respects TZ env var natively for all local date methods (getFullYear, getMonth, getDate, etc.) but toISOString() is always UTC regardless.

title: Tests for recipe import service
description: Unit tests for the URL import service: valid recipe page with JSON-LD, page with no recipe schema, network error, malformed JSON-LD, missing required fields. Mock fetch. Test field extraction accuracy.

title: Recipe import UI
description: Add 'Import from URL' button to DishesPage. Opens modal: URL input -> loading state -> preview parsed dish fields (editable) -> Save button that pre-fills the DishForm. Allow user to adjust before saving.

title: Parse recipe from URL (API)
description: POST /api/dishes/import-url - fetch URL, extract schema.org Recipe JSON-LD, return parsed dish fields (name, description, ingredients, instructions, prepTime, cookTime, servings, sourceUrl). Does not save to DB - returns preview data. Handle errors: non-recipe pages, fetch failures, parse failures. Library: use cheerio for HTML + custom JSON-LD extractor.

title: M8: Recipe Import from URL
description: Import recipes from URLs using schema.org Recipe JSON-LD parsing. Allow users to paste a recipe URL and have dish data pre-populated automatically.

title: Browser refresh redirects to login page
description: When using browser refresh (F5 or reload button), the user is redirected to the login page even though they were logged in. Pull-to-refresh gesture works correctly. This suggests an issue with authentication state initialization on page load or refresh token persistence.
close_reason: Root cause: POST /api/auth/refresh was sending Content-Type: application/json with an empty body. Fastify rejected with 400 FST_ERR_CTP_EMPTY_JSON_BODY. Fixed request() in api.ts to only add Content-Type header when a body is present.

title: Adding a dish doesn't persist to database
description:
When creating a new dish through the UI, the dish appears to be added but doesn't persist to the database. After refresh or navigation, the dish is no longer visible.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Navigate to Dishes page
2. Click to add a new dish
3. Fill in dish details (name, description, etc.)
4. Submit the form
5. Observe dish appears in the list
6. Refresh page or navigate away and back
7. Observe dish is no longer in the list

Expected behavior:
- Dish should be saved to database
- Dish should persist across page refreshes
- Dish should be visible after navigation

Actual behavior:
- Dish appears temporarily in UI
- Dish is not persisted to database
- Dish disappears on refresh

Possible causes:
- Create mutation not calling API correctly
- API endpoint not saving to database
- Database transaction not committing
- Optimistic update without proper server sync
- React Query cache issue not triggering actual API call
- Missing or incorrect API route handler
close_reason: Root cause: z.coerce.boolean() in dishQuerySchema converted string 'false' to boolean true (any non-empty string is truthy). GET /api/dishes?archived=false was returning archived=true dishes instead, so the list always appeared empty. Fixed with z.preprocess to compare string === 'true'.

title: Pull-to-refresh causes logout/redirect to login screen
description:
When using pull-to-refresh on list pages (DishesPage, HistoryPage, WeekPage), the action causes the user to be logged out and redirected to the login screen instead of refreshing the data.

This appears to be a critical bug affecting the core pull-to-refresh functionality that was recently implemented in the Mobile UX Enhancements.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Log in to the app on a mobile device
2. Navigate to any list page (Dishes, History, or Week)
3. Pull down to trigger pull-to-refresh
4. Observe unexpected logout

Expected behavior:
- Data should refresh
- User should remain on the same page
- User should remain authenticated

Actual behavior:
- User is logged out
- User is redirected to login screen

Possible causes:
- Pull-to-refresh onRefresh handler may be calling wrong API
- Auth token may be getting cleared
- Error in refetch logic causing auth failure
- React Query mutation may be affecting auth state
close_reason: Fixed by removing hard redirect from API client and adding error handling to pull-to-refresh. Now handles 401 errors gracefully through auth state instead of forcing navigation.

title: Mobile UX enhancements
description: Pull to refresh. Swipe actions on list items. Improved touch targets. Better mobile navigation.
close_reason: All 6 phases implemented: touch targets, pull-to-refresh, swipe actions on DishesPage/HistoryPage/WeekPage, safe area insets, haptic feedback. All 80 tests passing.

title: Add loading and error states
description: Loading skeletons for pages. Error boundary components. Toast notification system. Empty state designs.
close_reason: Skeleton, ErrorState, EmptyState, ErrorBoundary components implemented and integrated into all pages (DishesPage, HistoryPage, WeekPage, AdminUsersPage). TodayPage was already using them. ErrorBoundary added to App.tsx.

title: Add CI/CD pipeline
description: GitHub Actions for lint/type-check/test. Run on PR and main branch. Test database migrations.

title: Add test infrastructure
description: Set up Vitest for API and web. Add test scripts to package.json. Create example test files. Document testing approach in README.

title: fix: pin undici override to 7.x to prevent jsdom breakage
description: pnpm.overrides.undici in package.json is '>=7.24.0', which allowed Renovate PR #171 to bump undici to 8.0.2. But jsdom@29.0.1 requires undici ^7.24.5 and imports 'undici/lib/handler/wrap-handler.js' by absolute path — a file that doesn't exist in undici 8.x. This crashes the vitest forks worker before any test runs. Fix: change package.json:37 from '>=7.24.0' to '^7.24.0', then run pnpm install to regenerate lockfile.

title: feat: detect unsupported social media URLs in recipe import
description: Facebook and other social media URLs always fail with a cryptic 'HTTP 400 fetching recipe URL' error because these sites block scraping. Add a domain blocklist in validateRecipeUrl() (apps/api/src/services/recipeImport.ts:65) so they fail fast with a helpful message before any network request. Domains to block: facebook.com, fb.com, fb.watch, instagram.com, tiktok.com, twitter.com, x.com, pinterest.com. Add tests in apps/api/src/__tests__/recipeImport.test.ts.

title: Fix: beads issues.jsonl not updating on git pull in existing clones
description:
Add a post-merge git hook that re-imports issues.jsonl into SQLite after every git pull/merge.

Root cause: older beads versions set the assume-unchanged bit on .beads/issues.jsonl, causing git pull to skip updating the on-disk file. Even when the file is updated, nothing triggers a SQLite re-import.

Changes:
1. Create .githooks/post-merge — clears assume-unchanged/skip-worktree bits, then runs 'bd sync --rebuild' (removes orphaned entries + adds new from JSONL). Soft-fails on errors.
2. Edit package.json simple-git-hooks config — add: "post-merge": "sh .githooks/post-merge"
3. Run pnpm install to register the hook via simple-git-hooks.

Confirmed: git ls-files -v .beads/issues.jsonl shows 'h' (assume-unchanged) on current machine. bd sync --rebuild is the correct command (not bd import, which doesn't exist in current beads version).

title: API tokens: Admin Settings UI
description: 1. New ApiTokensSection.tsx component: table showing name/created/last used/expires + Revoke button per row. 2. New CreateTokenModal.tsx: name input + optional expiry date + on success show token once with copy button + 'will not be shown again' warning. 3. Add section to AdminSettingsPage.tsx. 4. TanStack Query hooks: useApiTokens (GET), useCreateApiToken (POST), useRevokeApiToken (DELETE /api/auth/tokens/:id).

title: API token management
description: Long-lived named API tokens for external access. Admin-only for now. dp_-prefixed tokens accepted on all authenticate-protected routes. Backend (DB migration, service, middleware, routes, tests) complete in PR #164. Frontend: Admin Settings UI section remaining.

title: migrate bd → br in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md
description: Mechanical migration: replace all bd commands with br equivalents. bd sync → br sync --flush-only + git steps. Branch: bd-dinner-planner-5tq (pushed, PR pending)

title: fix(web): per-entry scale — remove GroceryPage scale, add scale control to WeekPage/TodayPage
description:
Move scale from a GroceryPage display filter to a per-entry control on WeekPage/TodayPage.

## GroceryPage cleanup
- Remove 'scale' useState (line ~546)
- Remove the 1×/2×/4× segmented button UI
- Remove scale prop from GroceryRow, CategorySection, StandingRow
- Remove scaleQuantity/formatQuantity/buildPlainText scale parameters — quantities from the API are already correctly scaled
- The plain-text export (clipboard copy) should just use item.quantity directly

## WeekPage / TodayPage
- Add a compact 1×/2×/4× scale control inline on each assembled entry card (visible only when type='assembled' and mainDish is set)
- On change: PATCH /api/entries/:id with { scale } (add scale to UpdateEntryData type in the page)
- Show a subtle badge/label (e.g. '2×') on the entry when scale > 1 so it's visible at a glance
- On optimistic/success: invalidate the groceries query so the list refreshes with new quantities

## lib/api.ts
- Add 'scale: number' to the DinnerEntry response type
- Add 'scale?: number' to the UpdateEntryData/updateEntry call signature

## Tests
- WeekPage.test.tsx or TodayPage.test.tsx: scale control renders for assembled entry; PATCH called with scale value
- GroceryPage.test.tsx: remove any tests that tested the scale selector UI

title: fix(api): per-entry scale — migration, schema, groceries aggregation
description:
Add scale to dinner entries at the DB and service layer.

1. Migration: CREATE new migration SQL adding 'scale INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1' to dinner_entries. Register in journal, generate snapshot.

2. schema.ts: Add 'scale: integer("scale").notNull().default(1)' to dinnerEntries table.

3. packages/shared/src/schemas/index.ts: Add 'scale: z.number().int().min(1).max(4).default(1)' to updateDinnerEntrySchema. Add scale to any entry response type if defined there.

4. services/menus.ts updateDinnerEntry: Accept and persist scale.

5. services/groceries.ts getWeekGroceries: Change ingredient aggregation to respect per-entry scale. Currently builds a unique dishId set — instead collect {dishId, scale} per entry (same dish can appear multiple times at different scales). When building IngredientSource list, multiply ingredient quantity by the entry scale. The aggregate function already sums quantities for duplicate ingredient names — this will correctly handle the same dish at different scales across two entries.

6. Tests:
   - Migration snapshot registered
   - groceries.test.ts: test that a dish planned at scale=2 doubles ingredient quantities; test that two entries of same dish at scale 1 and scale 2 sum to 3x
   - menus.test.ts or integration.test.ts: PATCH /api/entries/:id with scale accepted and returned

Keep the scaleQuantity/formatQuantity helper in groceries.ts service if useful, but remove any display-time scaling from the API response — quantities returned are already correctly scaled.

title: bug: serving size scale should be per dinner entry, not a grocery list display filter
description:
## Current behavior
The scale selector (1×/2×/4×) lives on the GroceryPage and applies a display-time multiplier to all grocery items at once. It resets on navigation.

## Expected behavior
Scale should be set per planned dinner entry — e.g. 'We're making chicken casserole for guests this week → 2×'. That entry's ingredients are then included in the grocery list at 2× quantity. Other dishes are unaffected.

## Why this matters
The current approach conflates 'I want to see everything doubled' with 'I'm actually making this dish for more people'. Grocery quantities need to reflect reality at list-generation time, not at display time.

## Design
- Add a `scale` integer column (default 1) to `dinner_entries` table (migration required)
- PATCH /api/entries/:id — accept `scale` field
- `getWeekGroceries` in menus.ts multiplies ingredient quantities by entry.scale when building the list
- Remove the scale selector from GroceryPage
- Add a scale control to the dish planning UI (WeekPage / TodayPage) next to each planned entry — small segmented 1×/2×/4× control inline with the dish card
- Custom grocery items and standing items are not scaled (they are not recipe-derived)
- Tests: migration snapshot, service unit test for scaled quantities, route test, frontend for scale control on WeekPage

title: feat(shared): add low_calorie to DIETARY_TAGS schema
description: In packages/shared/src/schemas/index.ts, add 'low_calorie' to the DIETARY_TAGS const array (after 'low_carb'). This is a one-line change. Run shared package build + any affected tests.

title: feat(web): add Low Calorie label to DishesPage
description: In apps/web/src/pages/DishesPage.tsx, add low_calorie: 'Low-Calorie' to the DIETARY_TAG_LABELS (or equivalent label map) around line 50. The filter dropdown and DishForm checkboxes both map over DIETARY_TAGS dynamically so no other changes needed. Update any tests referencing the dietary tag list. Build to confirm TypeScript compiles.

title: feat: add Low Calorie dietary tag
description: Add 'low_calorie' as a new dietary tag option.

title: fix: CI test failures from missing mocks and DB import chain
description:
Three test suites fail in CI because better-sqlite3 native bindings can't load (they hit the real DB instead of mocks).

Root causes:
1. inferCategory.test.ts imports from dishes.ts which has top-level 'import { db } from db/index.js' — no DB mock in that test. Fix: move CATEGORY_RULES + inferCategory into a new src/services/categoryHeuristics.ts (no DB dependency), update dishes.ts and recipeImport.ts to import from there, update inferCategory.test.ts import path.
2. groceryRoutes.test.ts mocks customGroceries, groceryChecks, stores — but NOT standingItems, which was added to grocery.ts in M26. Fix: add vi.mock('../services/standingItems.js', ...) with listStandingItems, addStandingItem, deleteStandingItem stubs.

Files to change:
- apps/api/src/services/categoryHeuristics.ts (NEW) — copy CATEGORY_RULES + inferCategory from dishes.ts
- apps/api/src/services/dishes.ts — remove CATEGORY_RULES + inferCategory definition (lines 6-290), add: import { inferCategory } from './categoryHeuristics.js';
- apps/api/src/services/recipeImport.ts — change import of inferCategory from './dishes.js' to './categoryHeuristics.js'
- apps/api/src/__tests__/inferCategory.test.ts — change import from '../services/dishes.js' to '../services/categoryHeuristics.js'
- apps/api/src/__tests__/groceryRoutes.test.ts — add vi.mock block after existing mocks: vi.mock('../services/standingItems.js', () => ({ listStandingItems: vi.fn(), addStandingItem: vi.fn(), deleteStandingItem: vi.fn() }))

recipeImport.ts pre-existing failure is unrelated and should be left alone.

title: M26: Standing grocery items
description: Items that always appear on every weekly grocery list regardless of meal plan. New standing_items table (id, name, quantity, unit, category, store_id). Injected into GET /api/menus/week/:date/groceries as standingItems[]. Check keys: standing::{id}, reset weekly. Dedicated Every Week section on GroceryPage with inline add/delete. Participates in store filter and category grouping.

title: feat(m26): standing items backend
description: Migration 0016: standing_items table. Schema in apps/api/src/db/schema.ts. Service: apps/api/src/services/standingItems.ts (listStanding, addStanding, deleteStanding). Routes: GET/POST /api/grocery/standing, DELETE /api/grocery/standing/:id. Inject into GET /api/menus/week/:date/groceries response as standingItems[]. Check key prefix: standing::{id}.

title: feat(m26): standing items frontend
description: Add StandingItem type and standing API methods to apps/web/src/lib/api.ts. Add Every Week section to GroceryPage with checkable rows (key: standing::{id}), inline AddStandingItemForm, and delete button. Section participates in store filter. useGroceryChecklist already handles arbitrary key prefixes.

title: Quick wins: category heuristics + serving size scaling
description: Two quick wins: (1) backend auto-assigns ingredient categories by name rules when user leaves category as Other; (2) frontend serving size scale selector on GroceryPage multiplies quantities for display.

title: feat: auto-infer ingredient category from name
description: In apps/api/src/services/dishes.ts, when saving an ingredient with category 'Other' (or unset), run inferCategory(name) to auto-detect from keyword rules. Also apply in apps/api/src/services/recipeImport.ts where category is hardcoded to 'Other'. Rules: Produce (apple, banana, lettuce, spinach, tomato, onion, garlic, pepper, carrot, potato, broccoli, mushroom, celery, cucumber, zucchini, lemon, lime, avocado, corn, pea, bean, squash, kale, cilantro, parsley, basil, ginger, scallion, shallot, leek, asparagus, artichoke, eggplant, fennel, radish, beet), Dairy (milk, cream, butter, cheese, yogurt, sour cream, egg, parmesan, mozzarella, cheddar, ricotta, brie, feta, gouda, whipping cream, heavy cream, half and half), Meat (chicken, beef, pork, lamb, turkey, bacon, sausage, ham, steak, ground beef, ground turkey, ground pork, brisket, ribs, loin, tenderloin, thigh, breast, drumstick, wing), Seafood (fish, salmon, tuna, shrimp, crab, lobster, scallop, clam, oyster, cod, tilapia, halibut, mahi, sardine, anchovy, squid, octopus), Bakery (bread, flour, yeast, bun, roll, bagel, croissant, pita, tortilla, naan, baguette, sourdough, whole wheat, breadcrumb), Frozen (frozen, ice cream), Pantry Staples (oil, vinegar, salt, pepper, sugar, honey, soy sauce, sauce, paste, stock, broth, rice, pasta, noodle, oat, cereal, canned, can of, jar of, dried, spice, herb, cumin, paprika, oregano, thyme, bay leaf, cinnamon, nutmeg, clove, allspice, turmeric, curry, chili powder, cayenne, mustard, ketchup, mayo, mayonnaise, ranch, dressing, syrup, jam, jelly, peanut butter, almond butter, chocolate, cocoa, vanilla, baking powder, baking soda, cornstarch, breadcrumb), Beverages (water, juice, wine, beer, coffee, tea, soda, broth, stock, coconut milk, almond milk, oat milk), Household (foil, wrap, bag, towel, soap, detergent, sponge). Match is case-insensitive substring. First matching category wins; if no match, keep 'Other'. Only auto-assign when category is 'Other' — never override an explicit user choice.

title: feat: serving size scale selector on GroceryPage
description: Add a scale selector to GroceryPage (apps/web/src/pages/GroceryPage.tsx). State: const [scale, setScale] = useState(1). UI: small segmented button group '1×  2×  4×' placed inline next to the store filter (or below it). Multiply item.quantity by scale before display in GroceryRow, PantryGroceryRow, and formatQuantity/buildPlainText. Also scale custom item quantities. Do not persist to server — pure display transform. Scale resets to 1× when the week changes.

title: M25 frontend: category selector + store filter on GroceryPage
description: Dish ingredient editor: add category dropdown + store multi-select (with inline creation) per ingredient. GroceryPage: (1) group items by category with collapsible sections, section shows item count; (2) Store filter dropdown — 'All stores' plus distinct store names, filters visible items. Category section order: Produce, Dairy, Meat, Seafood, Bakery, Frozen, Pantry Staples, Beverages, Household, Other.

title: M25 backend: category + store fields on ingredients
description: DB migrations: (1) add category enum column to ingredients table (default 'Other', enum: Produce/Dairy/Meat/Seafood/Bakery/Pantry Staples/Frozen/Beverages/Household/Other); (2) create stores table (id, name unique, createdAt) + ingredientStores junction (ingredientId FK, storeId FK, composite PK). Custom grocery items (from M23) also get optional storeId FK. GET groceries returns category and stores[] per item. New endpoints: GET /api/stores, POST /api/stores {name}. Ingredient update/create schemas updated.

title: M24 backend: groceryChecks table and check toggle API
description: DB migration: groceryChecks(id, weekDate, itemName, checkedAt, checkedByUserId FK). GET /api/menus/week/:date/groceries returns checked:boolean per item. POST /api/grocery/checks {weekDate, itemName} to check, DELETE /api/grocery/checks/:id to uncheck. Must work for both dish-derived and custom items by name.

title: M24 frontend: server-backed check state with 5s polling
description: Replace useGroceryChecklist localStorage hook with server-backed state. Optimistic UI: toggle locally immediately, sync to server. Poll GET groceries every 5s while page is visible (use Page Visibility API to pause when backgrounded). Show who last checked each item (optional tooltip).

title: M23 backend: customGroceryItems table and CRUD API
description: DB migration: customGroceryItems(id, weekDate, name, quantity nullable, unit nullable, sortOrder, createdAt). Endpoints: POST /api/grocery/custom, PATCH /api/grocery/custom/:id, DELETE /api/grocery/custom/:id. GET /api/menus/week/:date/groceries updated to include custom items in response.

title: M23 frontend: Add/remove custom items on GroceryPage
description: Add item input at bottom of grocery list. Custom items render alongside dish-derived items (visually distinguished). Delete button on custom items. POST/DELETE to API on add/remove.
close_reason: Custom items UI + Add form + Delete + 630 tests, a11y fixes applied

title: M25: Grocery Organization (categories + store filtering)
description:
Two organizational features for the grocery list:

CATEGORY GROUPING: Add category field to ingredients (enum: Produce, Dairy, Meat, Seafood, Bakery, Pantry Staples, Frozen, Beverages, Household, Other — default Other). Users assign per ingredient in dish form. Grocery page groups items by category with collapsible sections.

STORE FILTERING: Some ingredients are only available at specific stores. Add store associations to ingredients (managed stores table + ingredientStores junction, or JSON array — TBD). Custom grocery items also get a store field. Grocery page gets a Store filter dropdown ('All' + distinct store names); items with no store assigned appear in all views. New store names created inline on first use.

Both features share the ingredient edit form in the dish editor and the GroceryPage filter/display UI.

title: M24: Shared Grocery Check State + Live Sync
description: Move check state from localStorage to server-side groceryChecks table (weekDate, itemName, checkedBy userId). Any family member checking off an item is visible to all. Frontend polls GET groceries every 5s while GroceryPage is active. Replaces useGroceryChecklist hook with server-backed state.

title: M23: Custom Grocery Items
description: Allow users to add free-form items to the grocery list beyond dish-derived ingredients. New customGroceryItems table scoped by weekDate. CRUD API endpoints. Add item UI on GroceryPage integrated alongside dish-derived items.

title: Replace release-please with workflow_dispatch release workflow
description:
PR #38 (bd-dinner-planner-ifb.1 branch). Remove release-please and add a manual release workflow:

REMOVE:
- .github/workflows/release-please.yml
- release-please-config.json
- .release-please-manifest.json

ADD .github/workflows/release.yml with:
- trigger: workflow_dispatch with input 'version' (e.g. v1.2.3, validated against semver pattern)
- steps: validate version format, update root package.json version field, commit + push, create git tag, create GitHub Release using gh CLI with --generate-notes flag
- The existing publish.yml already triggers on v*.*.* tags, so the image publish is automatic after the tag is created

Work in existing worktree .worktrees/bd-dinner-planner-ifb.1 on branch bd-dinner-planner-ifb.1. Push when done.

title: M14 frontend review fixes
description:
Fix 2 Copilot review issues on PR #37 (feature/m14-cook-log branch):

1. apps/web/src/lib/api.ts:591 — Remove the local DishNote interface entirely. Import and use the DishNote type from @dinner-planner/shared instead. Align all usages in api.ts to use the shared type.

2. apps/web/src/components/DishNotes.tsx:86 — Guard null createdByUsername: use note.createdByUsername ?? 'Unknown user' instead of rendering the value directly.

All changes on branch feature/m14-cook-log. Run tests after changes. Push to origin/feature/m14-cook-log when done.

title: M14 backend review fixes
description:
Fix 4 Copilot review issues on PR #37 (feature/m14-cook-log branch):

1. apps/api/src/routes/dishNotes.ts:46 — DELETE endpoint must verify requesting user is creator of note OR is admin before deleting. Check note.createdById === request.user.id (or admin role). Return 403 if unauthorized.

2. apps/api/src/routes/dishNotes.ts:32 — POST route returns note directly but frontend expects { note } wrapper. Change to reply.status(201).send({ note }).

3. packages/shared/src/schemas/index.ts:192 — Add .trim() to note field: z.string().trim().min(1).max(2000)

4. apps/api/src/db/schema.ts:150 — Add onDelete: 'set null' to createdById FK reference. Then run pnpm db:generate to create a new migration. Run pnpm db:migrate (or test migration) to verify.

All changes on branch feature/m14-cook-log. Run existing tests after changes to ensure nothing is broken (327 tests should pass). Push to origin/feature/m14-cook-log when done.

title: M14 PR review fixes
description: Address 6 Copilot review comments on PR #37 before merging M14

title: Add GHCR publish workflow
description:
Add publish.yml GitHub Actions workflow:
- Triggers on push to tags matching v* (created by release-please)
- Logs in to ghcr.io using GITHUB_TOKEN
- Builds optimized Docker image (from ifb.1 Dockerfile) and pushes to ghcr.io/duckpuppy/dinner-planner
- Tags: latest + semver tag (e.g. v1.2.3)
- After push, use actions/delete-package-versions to retain only the 3 most recent versions (manages 500MB storage constraint)
- Works in same worktree as ifb.1 and ifb.2

title: Add release-please workflow
description:
Add release-please-action@v4 GitHub Actions workflow:
- Uses conventional commits (already in use) to generate CHANGELOG and bump versions
- Creates 'Release PR' targeting main; on merge creates GitHub Release + tag
- Add .release-please-manifest.json and release-please-config.json
- Works in same worktree as ifb.1 (feature/release-workflow)

title: Optimize Dockerfile for slim production image
description:
Refactor Dockerfile to produce the smallest possible image:
- Use pnpm deploy --filter=@dinner-planner/api --prod to get only API production deps
- Rebuild native modules (bcrypt, better-sqlite3) in builder stage where build tools exist, avoiding layer bloat in runner
- Do NOT copy apps/web/node_modules or packages/shared/node_modules to runner (web is pre-built static files, shared is compiled)
- Root node_modules dev tooling also excluded
- Target: image under 200MB
- Branch: feature/release-workflow off main (create worktree at .worktrees/release-workflow)
- Note: CMD must still run migrations then serve; API serves web/dist as static files so web/dist must be in the image

title: Release workflow and Docker image optimization
description: Add release-please for automated releases, optimize Dockerfile with pnpm deploy pattern, and publish images to GHCR on release. Targeted at staying under 500MB GHCR storage on free tier.

title: JWT_SECRET startup validation

close_reason: Already implemented — config.ts:9 validates JWT_SECRET min 32 chars and exits on failure

title: Auth cookie flags audit & fix
description: Audit refresh token cookie flags: confirm httpOnly, SameSite=Strict (or Lax), and Secure (in production). Check apps/api/src/routes/auth.ts. No domain attribute (already fixed). Ensure Secure flag is set when NODE_ENV=production.
close_reason: Implemented: sameSite upgraded to strict in auth.ts:12. PR #31

title: Input validation audit (Zod maxLength, no raw SQL)
description: Audit all Zod schemas in packages/shared/src/schemas/index.ts and inline route validation — ensure every string field has a maxLength. Grep for any raw SQL or string interpolation in query construction. Fix any gaps found.
close_reason: Implemented: max(128) on all password fields in shared/schemas/index.ts. PR #31

title: SSRF protection on recipe import
description: Harden apps/api/src/services/recipeImport.ts URL validation: block private/loopback IPs (127.x, 10.x, 192.168.x, ::1), require https scheme only, enforce response size limit (e.g. 5MB). Use dns.lookup to resolve and check the target IP before fetching.

title: Rate limiting on auth and import endpoints
description: Install @fastify/rate-limit and apply limits: POST /api/auth/login (10 req/min per IP), POST /api/auth/refresh (20 req/min), POST /api/dishes/import-url (5 req/min). Return 429 with Retry-After header.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #33 (security/rate-limiting), depends on PR #32

title: Security headers (@fastify/helmet + CSP)
description: Install @fastify/helmet and register it in apps/api/src/server.ts. Configure a Content-Security-Policy appropriate for the Fastify API (no UI served from API). Review defaults and tighten where possible.

title: pnpm audit CI gate (high/critical = 0)
description: Add a pnpm audit --audit-level=high step to the GitHub Actions CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml). Fail the build if any high or critical vulnerabilities are found. Run pnpm audit now and fix any existing issues first.

title: M11: Frontend component tests — PatternsPage and WeekPage
description: Add React Testing Library tests for new M9/M10 frontend components. PatternsPage (apps/web/src/pages/PatternsPage.tsx): renders empty state when no patterns, renders patterns grouped by day of week, opens create form on button click, calls delete API on confirm. WeekPage dining-out inputs (apps/web/src/pages/WeekPage.tsx): shows restaurant name + notes fields when type=dining_out, hides them for other types, apply-patterns button calls applyToWeek mutation. RecipeImportModal (apps/web/src/components/RecipeImportModal.tsx): URL input present, shows loading state during fetch, calls onImported with parsed recipe on success, shows error message on failure. Mock @/lib/api with vi.mock. Follow patterns in apps/web/src/pages/DishForm.test.tsx and apps/web/src/components/SuggestionModal.test.tsx.

title: M11: API integration tests
description: Add HTTP-level integration tests using Fastify's inject() API (no real HTTP server needed). Test file: apps/api/src/__tests__/integration/. Use in-memory SQLite and a test Fastify app instance. Coverage: (1) Auth — POST /api/auth/login success+failure, POST /api/auth/refresh, POST /api/auth/logout; (2) Patterns CRUD — GET/POST /api/patterns, GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/patterns/:id, POST /api/menus/week/:date/apply-patterns; (3) Recipe import — POST /api/dishes/import-url with mocked fetch (valid JSON-LD 201, non-recipe page 422, network error 422); (4) Dishes — GET /api/dishes with archived filter, DELETE /api/dishes/:id cascade. Use vitest beforeEach to seed minimal test data (admin user + auth token).

title: M11: Unit tests — dishes and menus services
description: Add unit tests for apps/api/src/services/dishes.ts and menus.ts. Use vitest + in-memory SQLite. dishes.ts cases: getDishes with archived=false filter, getDishes with search query, getDishes with tag filter, deleteDish cascades (nullifies mainDishId on dinnerEntries, deletes junction rows, deletes preparations+ratings). menus.ts cases: getOrCreateWeekMenu creates new menu when none exists, getOrCreateWeekMenu returns existing menu on second call, updateDinnerEntry sets restaurantName+restaurantNotes for dining_out type.

title: M11: Unit tests — patterns service
description: Add unit tests for apps/api/src/services/patterns.ts. Test file: apps/api/src/__tests__/patterns.test.ts. Use vitest + in-memory SQLite (same pattern as existing groceries.test.ts). Cases: (1) listPatterns - empty, with data; (2) createPattern - assembled with dishes, dining_out with customText; (3) updatePattern - partial update, no field reset; (4) deletePattern - removes DB row; (5) applyPatternsToWeek - no patterns (no-op), untouched entries get pattern applied, entries with mainDishId skipped, entries with side dishes skipped, entries with customText skipped, dining_out pattern maps customText to restaurantName, deterministic selection when multiple patterns match same day.

title: M11: Configure coverage thresholds and CI gate
description: @vitest/coverage-v8 is already installed in both apps. Need to: (1) Add coverage config to apps/api/vitest.config.ts (provider: v8, thresholds: lines 80, functions 80, branches 70); (2) Add matching coverage config to web test config in apps/web/vite.config.ts (provider: v8, thresholds: lines 70, functions 70); (3) Add test:coverage scripts to both package.json files; (4) Add coverage run to .github/workflows/ci.yml (pnpm test:coverage --reporter=json, fail on threshold breach). Epic: dinner-planner-6cp

title: M12: Security Hardening
description: Audit and harden the app before broader deployment. Key areas: rate limiting on auth + import endpoints (@fastify/rate-limit), security headers (@fastify/helmet with CSP), SSRF protection on recipe import URL (block private IPs, https-only, size limit), input validation audit (maxLength on all Zod schemas, no raw SQL), auth cookie flags review (httpOnly/SameSite/Secure), JWT_SECRET env validation at startup, pnpm audit CI gate (high/critical = 0).
close_reason: All 7 security hardening tasks complete. PRs #31 (cookie/validation), #32 (helmet/SSRF/rate-limits/audit CI) merged to main.

title: M11: Test Coverage
description: Achieve meaningful automated test coverage across API services and frontend components. Key gaps: recipeImport.ts parser tests, patterns service tests (applyPatternsToWeek), API integration tests (auth/patterns/dishes), frontend tests for PatternsPage/WeekPage dining-out/RecipeImportModal. Targets: API services 80%+ line coverage, frontend 70%+, CI gate on thresholds.

description: Update the ingredient display in DishesPage dish detail view to show properly formatted ingredients: '2 cups flour', '1 tbsp olive oil (extra virgin)', etc. Currently displays raw name only since quantity/unit are not saved.
close_reason: Display was already correct; b5t completing the data layer makes it work end-to-end.

title: Structured ingredient row editor
description: Replace the freeform 'one ingredient per line' textarea in the dish form with a proper row-based editor. Each row has: quantity (number input), unit (text, e.g. 'cup', 'tbsp'), name (required), notes (optional). Rows can be added, removed, and reordered (up/down buttons or drag). On save, properly populate the structured ingredients schema (quantity, unit, name, notes, sortOrder). Update ingredient display in the dish detail view to show '2 cups flour' format.

title: [MILESTONE] M7: Recipe Enhancement
description: Upgrade the dish entry form to support full structured recipe data. Ingredients currently save as name-only (quantity/unit discarded). Replace the freeform textarea with row-based ingredient entry that properly stores quantity, unit, name, and notes per ingredient. All other recipe fields (instructions, prep/cook time, servings, source/video URL) are already implemented in the form and schema.
close_reason: All M7 tasks complete: structured ingredient editor (b5t), ingredient display (731 already done), tag chip editor (ogy).

title: Configure PWA manifest and icons
description: Web app manifest. Install prompt. App icons for multiple sizes.
close_reason: PWA manifest configured via vite-plugin-pwa. Icons generated: pwa-192x192.png, pwa-512x512.png, maskable-icon-512x512.png, apple-touch-icon. InstallPrompt component for manual install. Apple PWA meta tags in index.html.

title: Build offline functionality
description: View current week offline. View cached recipes offline. Offline indicator banner. Queue changes made offline.
close_reason: OfflineBanner shows 'showing cached data' banner when offline. TanStack Query offlineFirst mode serves IDB-cached data when offline. Mutations queue when offline and auto-resume on reconnect.

title: Implement sync mechanism
description: Sync queued changes when online. Conflict resolution (server wins, notify user). Sync status indicator.
close_reason: useOfflineSync hook: syncs onlineManager with browser events, shows 'Back online — syncing changes' toast, invalidates all queries on reconnect. TanStack Query automatically replays paused mutations.

title: Add IndexedDB data caching
description: Cache current week's menu in IndexedDB. Cache referenced dishes/recipes. Sync endpoint for bulk data fetch.
close_reason: Custom IndexedDB persister using idb-keyval. Hydrates TanStack Query cache on startup. Debounced cache writes on query success. 24h max age. networkMode: offlineFirst on all queries and mutations.

title: Implement service worker
description: Cache static assets (JS, CSS, images). App shell caching strategy. Offline detection.
close_reason: Service worker via Workbox (vite-plugin-pwa). Precaches all static assets. Network-first strategy for /api/ with 24h cache, 5s timeout, 100-entry limit. useOnlineStatus hook for offline detection.

title: [MILESTONE] M4: Offline Support (PWA)
description: Milestone 4 tracking issue. Enable offline access to current week's menu and recipes. Deliverables: Installable PWA, Works offline for current week, Reliable sync when back online.
close_reason: All M4 deliverables: installable PWA, offline read via IDB cache + service worker, sync on reconnect via TanStack Query paused mutations + query invalidation.

title: Desktop layout improvements
description: Sidebar navigation (desktop). Multi-column layouts where appropriate. Responsive breakpoints.
close_reason: Sidebar: user avatar, display name, and sign out button added. DishesPage: 2-column grid on desktop. All pages use max-w containers for proper reading width.

title: Implement hard delete for dishes
description: Admin-only delete action. Confirmation dialog with impact warning. Handle orphaned references in history.

title: Add home view preference
description: User setting for default view (today/week). Persist to DB. Redirect on login to preferred view.

title: Add app settings page
description: Configure week start day (Sun/Mon). Persist to app settings table. Apply throughout app.

title: Build user management screen
description: List all users with roles. Edit user (name, role). Reset user password (admin only). Delete user (admin only).

title: Implement theme system
description: Add theme context/store. Light/dark mode toggle. Persist user theme preference to DB. Apply Catppuccin Macchiato dark theme.

title: [MILESTONE] M3: User Experience Polish
description: Milestone 3 tracking issue. Add user preferences, admin settings, and UX improvements. Deliverables: Full theme support, Complete admin functionality, Polished user experience.
close_reason: All M3 deliverables complete: theme system, home view preference, app settings, user management, hard delete, loading/error states, mobile UX (pull-to-refresh, swipe actions, haptics, touch targets), desktop layout (sidebar user info, multi-column dishes), deployment docs, API docs.

title: streamline grocery page UX for mobile and desktop
description: Remove inline add forms from GroceryPage. Replace AddCustomItemForm with 'Add item' button → AddCustomItemDialog. Replace AddStandingItemForm with 'Manage recurring items' button → ManageStandingItemsDialog. Standing items stay visible inline. Consistent across all device sizes. Branch: bd-dinner-planner-qhm.1 (pushed, PR pending merge)

title: epic: UX review and improvement
description: Holistic UX review across all pages. Identify friction points, mobile usability issues, and visual consistency gaps. Each finding becomes a child feature under this epic.

title: epic: streamline grocery page UX for mobile
description: The grocery page currently has three distinct item types (recipe-derived ingredients, standing items, custom items) displayed in separate sections. On mobile this feels cluttered. Design and implement a more unified, mobile-friendly grocery list experience. Specifics TBD — may include unified item rows, collapsible sections, reordering, or a flat sorted list with type indicators.
close_reason: replaced by dinner-planner-q1g.1 — implementation branch bd-dinner-planner-qhm.1 pushed and pending PR

title: Portion scaling - UI
description: In dish detail view (DishesPage dish detail modal/panel), add a servings scaler input. When user changes servings from the dish's default, recalculate and display ingredient quantities scaled proportionally. Null quantities show a warning. No API changes needed - purely client-side math.
close_reason: Portion scaling UI implemented in DishDetail — +/- servings scaler, scaled quantities, null-quantity warnings

title: Photo uploads - frontend
description: In preparation detail view (HistoryPage), add photo upload button. Show uploaded photos as thumbnails in a grid. Allow deletion of own photos. Show photos in TodayPage preparation card.
close_reason: PreparationPhotos component added to HistoryPage and TodayPage; photos API namespace in api.ts

title: Photo uploads - storage + API
description: Add file upload infrastructure. Store photos in apps/api/data/uploads/ (served as /uploads/). Add photos table: id, preparation_id (FK), filename, mime_type, size, uploaded_at. POST /api/preparations/:id/photos (multipart form), GET /api/preparations/:id/photos, DELETE /api/photos/:id. Use @fastify/multipart.
close_reason: Photos DB table, service, routes + @fastify/multipart + /uploads/ static serving

title: M10: Visual & Scaling Enhancements
description: Photo uploads for preparations and portion scaling for dish ingredients.
close_reason: M10 complete: portion scaling in DishDetail + photo uploads on preparations

title: Tags comma-separated input → pill chip editor
description: Optional UX improvement: replace the comma-separated tags text input with interactive pill/chip input (type and press Enter to add, click X to remove). Makes tag entry less error-prone.

title: Create suggestion UI and refinement
description: Suggest a meal button in entry editor. Show suggested dishes with reasoning. Quick-add from suggestion. Filter suggestions by tag. Not this to skip suggestion.

title: Build suggestion algorithm
description: Weight dishes by aggregate rating. Penalize recently prepared dishes. Configurable recency window. Exclude archived dishes.

title: [MILESTONE] M6: Meal Suggestions
description: Milestone 6 tracking issue. Implement intelligent meal suggestions based on ratings and recency. Deliverables: Working meal suggestion feature, Suggestions consider ratings and recency.
close_reason: M6 complete: meal suggestion algorithm + UI

title: Prepare app store submission materials
description: iOS App Store submission materials. Google Play submission materials. Privacy policy page.
close_reason: No store submission needed. iPhone uses PWA, Android uses sideloaded APK.

title: Add native features (haptics, share)
description: Enhance haptics for Capacitor Android using @capacitor/haptics. Web Vibration API already implemented in M3.

title: Integrate Capacitor for iOS/Android
description: Integrate Capacitor for Android only. Generate sideloadable debug APK. iOS handled by PWA.

title: [MILESTONE] M5: Mobile App
description: Milestone 5 tracking issue. Package as native mobile app for app store distribution. Deliverables: iOS app (TestFlight/App Store), Android app (Play Store).
close_reason: M5 complete: Android APK via Capacitor + CI build; iPhone via existing M4 PWA

title: Add API documentation
description: Generate OpenAPI/Swagger docs. Document authentication flow. Add example requests/responses.
close_reason: docs/api.md: auth flow, endpoint summary table with all routes. Interactive Swagger UI via @fastify/swagger + @fastify/swagger-ui at /docs in development.

title: Complete deployment docs
description: Docker deployment guide. Environment variable reference. Production checklist. Backup/restore procedures.
close_reason: docs/deployment.md: Docker quick start, env var reference, production checklist, backup/restore. Swagger UI at /docs in dev mode.
